narration: You check your portfolio every morning thinking you're being responsible. That one daily habit is quietly costing you forty-seven thousand dollars.
visual: Dark background, phone with animated stock chart line drawing across the screen, portfolio value '$142,830' displayed, then amber badge pops in reading 'THIS HABIT COSTS YOU $47K' with counter

narration: DALBAR tracked thirty years of real investor returns. Average investors earn just two point nine percent per year. The S&P 500 earns seven point five. Your behavior explains the whole difference.
visual: Light background, two horizontal bars growing — short red bar 'AVERAGE INVESTOR 2.9%/YR' and long green bar 'S&P 500 INDEX 7.5%/YR', DALBAR source label, '4.6% BEHAVIOR GAP' text springs in

narration: Here's what that gap does to a hundred thousand dollars over thirty years. Checking daily? Two hundred thirty-eight thousand. Hands-off? Eight hundred forty thousand. Over six hundred grand gone.
visual: Dark background, two horizontal bars growing — red bar reaching '$238K' and green bar reaching '$840K' — large amber '$602K' difference counter animates in at bottom

narration: See red in your portfolio and loss aversion kicks in — your brain feels losses twice as hard as gains. So you panic-sell at the worst possible moment, every single time.
visual: Light background, illustrated brain character with worried face, two side-by-side boxes: red '2X WORSE' for losses vs green '1X NORMAL' for gains, overlay text 'you panic-sell on red days'

narration: Monthly checkers outperform daily checkers by two percent every year. That's not discipline — it's just not being around to wreck your own returns. Let compounding do the work.
visual: Dark background, calendar icon with 'CHECK ONCE A MONTH' label and large checkmark, '+2%/YR' stat badge springs in, amber compound growth bar fills right showing '$602K MORE'

narration: Three moves to fix it: delete the investment app, check only quarterly, and automate your contributions. That shift puts six hundred thousand dollars back in your future pocket.
visual: Light background, three step cards slide in sequentially — phone with X '1. DELETE THE APP', calendar '2. CHECK QUARTERLY', gear icon '3. AUTOMATE BUYS' — pulsing amber FOLLOW FOR MORE button
